title: planning
description: Parent epic for CAO + Beads integration tasks from PRD_BEADS_INTEGRATION and CAO_BEADS_ORCHESTRATION_PRD

title: Create BeadsClient Wrapper for Real Beads Integration
description:
Implement a working BeadsClient wrapper that integrates with the real Beads library (beads-mcp / bd CLI) instead of the custom SQLite implementation.

## Background
CAO currently has a simple BeadsClient using SQLite. The real Beads library is installed (bd CLI v0.48.0, beads-mcp v0.48.0) and initialized in the project.

## Reference Documentation
Required: .sop/planning/PRD_BEADS_INTEGRATION.md
acceptance_criteria:
1. BeadsClient Interface - Given existing interface, When wrapper implemented, Then all methods work
2. CLI Output Parsing - Given bd create output, When parsing, Then task ID extracted correctly
3. Priority/Status Mapping - Given CAO formats, When converting, Then mappings correct (1→P1, wip↔in_progress)
4. API Integration - Given web.py import updated, Then /api/tasks/* endpoints work

Unit Tests: list/get/add/update/delete, priority/status mapping, CLI parsing
Demo: Tasks via API visible in bd list and vice versa
notes:
## Patterns
- BeadsClient class wraps bd CLI via subprocess
- Task dataclass: id, title, description, priority, status, assignee, timestamps
- _run_bd() helper: subprocess.run with --no-daemon flag
- _parse_create_output(): regex to extract ID from 'Created issue: xyz'
- _parse_json(): json.loads with empty list fallback

## Dependencies
- subprocess, json, re, dataclasses (stdlib)

## Integration
- web.py line 9: from cli_agent_orchestrator.clients.beads import BeadsClient, Task
- v2.py line 17: from cli_agent_orchestrator.clients.beads import BeadsClient

## Key Mappings
- Priority: CAO 1→P1, 2→P2, 3→P3; Beads P0/P1→1, P2→2, P3/P4→3
- Status: CAO wip↔Beads in_progress

title: Phase 1: Foundation - Assignment Modal and Session Hierarchy
description:
Add orchestrator mode toggle to assignment modal and implement basic session hierarchy tracking.

## Background
UI supports single agent assignment. This adds orchestrator mode selection and parent-child session relationships.

## Reference Documentation
Required: .sop/planning/CAO_BEADS_ORCHESTRATION_PRD.md (Phase 1)
acceptance_criteria:
1. Assignment Modal - Given Assign clicked, When modal opens, Then Single Agent and Orchestrator modes shown
2. Session Parent Tracking - Given supervisor spawns workers, When parent_session set, Then hierarchy tracked
3. Sessions Panel Hierarchy - Given parent-child sessions, When viewing panel, Then workers nested under supervisors
4. Activity Feed - Given orchestration events, When viewing feed, Then events shown

Unit Tests: modal toggle, session relationships, activity events
Demo: Orchestrator mode selectable, sessions show hierarchy
notes:
## Technical Requirements
- Add mode toggle to AssignmentModal, parent_session to Session model
- Update SessionsPanel for hierarchy, add orchestration events

## Dependencies: BeadsClient Wrapper

Complexity: Medium | Skills: React/TypeScript

title: Phase 2: Hierarchy - Sub-bead Creation and Bead Hierarchy Display
description:
Implement sub-bead creation (bd-xxx.1 format) and display bead hierarchy in BeadsPanel.

## Background
Beads supports hierarchical IDs mapping to orchestrator task decomposition.

## Reference Documentation
Required: .sop/planning/CAO_BEADS_ORCHESTRATION_PRD.md (Phase 2)
acceptance_criteria:
1. Sub-bead Creation - Given parent bead, When decomposed, Then sub-beads have hierarchical IDs
2. Bead Hierarchy Display - Given parent-child beads, When viewing BeadsPanel, Then children nested
3. Session-Bead Links - Given assignment, When viewing either, Then link shown
4. Dependency Tracking - Given blocked_by/blocks, When viewing bead, Then dependencies displayed

Unit Tests: sub-bead creation, hierarchy rendering, dependency storage
Demo: Sub-beads created, tree structure visible
notes:
## Technical Requirements
- POST /api/v2/beads/{id}/decompose endpoint
- parent_bead/child_beads fields, tree view component

## Dependencies: Phase 1 Foundation

Complexity: Medium | Skills: React/TypeScript, API design

title: Phase 3: Communication - Messages Panel and Inbox
description:
Create Messages panel for inter-agent communication with real-time updates.

## Background
CAO send_message mode needs visibility into message queue and inbox status.

## Reference Documentation
Required: .sop/planning/CAO_BEADS_ORCHESTRATION_PRD.md (Phase 3)
acceptance_criteria:
1. Messages Panel - Given agent messages, When viewing tab, Then messages shown with from/to/status
2. Inbox Count - Given pending messages, When viewing Sessions, Then count shown
3. Real-time Updates - Given new message, When panel open, Then appears via WebSocket
4. Message Filtering - Given many messages, When filtering, Then results filtered

Unit Tests: message rendering, inbox count, WebSocket updates, filtering
Demo: Messages panel works, inbox counts visible, real-time updates
notes:
## Technical Requirements
- InboxMessage model, GET /api/v2/sessions/{id}/inbox
- Messages tab, WebSocket subscription, filter controls

## Dependencies: Phase 2 Hierarchy

Complexity: High | Skills: React/TypeScript, WebSocket

title: Phase 4: Visualization - Orchestration Flow View
description:
Create Orchestrations panel with visual flow diagrams and bulk operations.

## Background
Users need visual representation of orchestrations showing supervisor-worker relationships.

## Reference Documentation
Required: .sop/planning/CAO_BEADS_ORCHESTRATION_PRD.md (Phase 4)
acceptance_criteria:
1. Orchestrations Panel - Given active orchestrations, When viewing tab, Then list shown
2. Flow Diagram - Given orchestration selected, When viewing, Then flow diagram shows relationships
3. Status Tracking - Given worker completion/failure, When viewing, Then status reflects state
4. Bulk Operations - Given running orchestration, When Stop All clicked, Then all stopped

Unit Tests: list rendering, flow diagram, status aggregation, bulk stop
Demo: Flow diagrams visible, status updates, bulk stop works
notes:
## Technical Requirements
- Orchestration model, POST/GET/DELETE /api/v2/orchestrations
- Flow diagram component (react-flow), status aggregation, bulk operations

## Dependencies: Phase 3 Communication

Complexity: High | Skills: React/TypeScript, data visualization
